runs.notes: Filter-method rule confirmed working. get_pending_queries() correctly excluded. State named 'Pending' vs GT 'Scheduled' — semantic equivalent (both = executed=FALSE). Cosmetic naming gap; structure correct.

All 6 cross-file calls captured including 4 instance-method calls on constructed sync_manager. KEY FINDING: chained-method-call gap is a GRAPH PARSER issue, not a skill issue. Skill reads source directly and correctly identifies all instance method calls. Alt block correct.

Perfect match. 3 classes, 1 edge (WeeklyIngredientAnalysis --> IngredientUsage). Correctly applied edge rule: IngredientAnalyzer.analyze_weekly_plan() returns WeeklyIngredientAnalysis but no edge drawn (return type, not field type).

GT: 0 edges. Skill drew 1 edge (AllergenIndexer --> AllergenHierarchy via pattern_map: Dict[str, AllergenHierarchy]). RULE GAP exposed: generic type parameter ambiguity. GT applied strict rule: field declared as Dict = built-in = no edge. Skill applied loose rule: AllergenHierarchy appears in Dict[str, AllergenHierarchy] = draw edge. hallucination = 1 extra edge / (2 nodes + 1 edge) = 0.33. Fix: add generic-type rule to SKILL.md.

GT: 15 nodes, 17 edges. Skill: 13 nodes, 16 edges. Missed __init__ and setup_logging (constructor -> logging setup pair). Minor gap: constructor-called-logging pattern not captured. All main pipeline nodes and edges present. No hallucination.

All 3 GT actors captured. All 8 GT calls present in skill. OVER-ELABORATION: skill added _get_local_sync_state (likely intra-file self.* call, not cross-file), 3 ISM->ISM self-messages (error return states), and 4 extra _get_connection() instances (same method, multiple call sites within conditional blocks). Extra items not in GT: ~4 out of 17 total = 0.24 hallucination. Fix: add sequenceDiagram anti-over-elaboration rules to SKILL.md.

new_gaps_identified.description: When a field is typed as a generic container (List[LocalClass], Dict[K, LocalClass]), the skill draws an edge to the local class. GT applied strict rule: container type (Dict) = built-in = no edge. Needs explicit SKILL.md rule: only draw edges when the TOP-LEVEL declared type is a local class, not a type parameter.
new_gaps_identified.skill_md_fix: Add to classDiagram rules: For generic containers (List[T], Dict[K,V], Optional[T], Set[T]): the container itself (List, Dict, etc.) is the declared type. Do NOT draw edges based on type parameters. AllergenIndexer.pattern_map: Dict[str, AllergenHierarchy] — declared type is Dict, NOT AllergenHierarchy. No edge.

new_gaps_identified.description: Skill adds intra-file calls (self._method(), self.*), ISM->ISM self-messages for error/return states, and multiple instances of the same repeated call site within loops/conditionals.
new_gaps_identified.skill_md_fix: Add to sequenceDiagram rules: (1) Never draw SelfActor->SelfActor arrows. Return/error states are internal — they are NOT cross-file calls. (2) For repeated calls to the same target method within a loop or multiple conditional branches, show ONE representative call (or use a loop block), not separate arrows for each invocation. (3) Self.* calls (self._helper()) are intra-file. Only self.<injected_dependency>.* calls are cross-file.

confirmed_findings.finding: The chained-method-call gap (manager = Foo(); manager.bar() not captured) is confirmed as a GRAPH PARSER limitation, not a skill limitation. The skill reads source code directly and correctly identifies all instance method calls as cross-file. Graph parsers (parser_python.py) may not resolve dynamic instance method calls to their defining files.
Filter-method rule (get_*, fetch_*, find_*, load_*, query_* are SQL observers) correctly prevents get_pending_queries() from being treated as a state transition in dummy_query_scheduler.py.
